info.title: Gafaelfawr
info.description: Gafaelfawr is a FastAPI application for the authorization and management of tokens, including their issuance and revocation.

get.description: Only information from LDAP is displayed. Queries for bot users will not contain any useful information unless they also exist in LDAP, and Gafaelfawr installations using GitHub for authentication will return 404 errors for all users since LDAP is not configured.

patch.description: Replace metadata of a user token with provided values. Only the token name, scope, and expiration may be changed. Only token administrators may modify tokens; users cannot modify even their own tokens and should instead create a new token and delete the old one.

schema.title: CSRF token
schema.description: Only required when authenticating with a cookie, such as via the JavaScript UI.

parameters.description: Only required when authenticating with a cookie, such as via the JavaScript UI.

get.description: All changes are returned. Pagination is not supported.

get.description: Meant to be used as an NGINX auth_request handler

parameters.name: allow_options

schema.title: Allow OPTIONS
schema.description: Whether to allow non-CORS OPTIONS requests

parameters.description: Whether to allow non-CORS OPTIONS requests

parameters.name: auth_type

schema.title: Challenge type
schema.description: Control the type of WWW-Authenticate challenge returned

parameters.description: Control the type of WWW-Authenticate challenge returned

parameters.name: delegate_to

schema.title: Service name
schema.description: Create an internal token for the named service

parameters.description: Create an internal token for the named service

parameters.name: delegate_scope

schema.title: Scope of delegated token
schema.description: Scopes to add to the delegated token if present in the token used for authentication

parameters.description: Scopes to add to the delegated token if present in the token used for authentication

parameters.name: minimum_lifetime

schema.title: Required minimum lifetime
schema.description: Force reauthentication if the delegated token (internal or notebook) would have a shorter lifetime, in seconds, than this parameter.

parameters.description: Force reauthentication if the delegated token (internal or notebook) would have a shorter lifetime, in seconds, than this parameter.

parameters.name: notebook

schema.title: Request notebook token
schema.description: Cannot be used with `delegate_to` or `delegate_scope`

parameters.description: Cannot be used with `delegate_to` or `delegate_scope`

parameters.name: only_service

schema.title: Restrict to service
schema.description: Restrict access to only tokens issued to the named service, in addition to any other constraints. This will prevent users from accessing the service directly, but allow the named service to access it on their behalf. May be given multiple times to allow multiple services.

parameters.description: Restrict access to only tokens issued to the named service, in addition to any other constraints. This will prevent users from accessing the service directly, but allow the named service to access it on their behalf. May be given multiple times to allow multiple services.

parameters.name: satisfy

schema.title: Scope matching policy
schema.description: Set to `all` to require all listed scopes, set to `any` to require any of the listed scopes

parameters.description: Set to `all` to require all listed scopes, set to `any` to require any of the listed scopes

parameters.name: scope

schema.title: Required scopes
schema.description: If given more than once, meaning is determined by the `satisfy` parameter

parameters.description: If given more than once, meaning is determined by the `satisfy` parameter

parameters.name: service

schema.title: Service
schema.description: Name of the underlying service

parameters.description: Name of the underlying service

parameters.name: use_authorization

schema.title: Put delegated token in Authorization
schema.description: If true, also replace the Authorization header with any delegated token, passed as a bearer token.

parameters.description: If true, also replace the Authorization header with any delegated token, passed as a bearer token.

parameters.name: user_domain

schema.title: Enforce per-user hostnames
schema.description: Restrict access to only the user whose username matches the last component of the request hostname.

parameters.description: Restrict access to only the user whose username matches the last component of the request hostname.

parameters.name: username

schema.title: Restrict to username
schema.description: Only allow access to this ingress by the user with the given username. All other users, regardless of scopes, will receive 403 errors. The user must still meet the scope requirements for the ingress.

parameters.description: Only allow access to this ingress by the user with the given username. All other users, regardless of scopes, will receive 403 errors. The user must still meet the scope requirements for the ingress.

parameters.name: allow_cookies

schema.title: Whether to allow cookies
schema.description: Set to false to disallow cookie authentication

parameters.description: Set to false to disallow cookie authentication

parameters.name: access-control-request-method

schema.description: Method for CORS preflight
schema.title: Access-Control-Request-Method

parameters.description: Method for CORS preflight

parameters.name: origin

schema.description: Origin header of original request
schema.title: Origin

parameters.description: Origin header of original request

parameters.name: x-original-method

schema.description: Request method
schema.title: X-Original-Method

parameters.description: Request method

schema.title: Response Get Auth Ingress Auth Get

get.description: Intended for use as an auth-url handler for anonymous routes. No authentication is done and no authorization checks are performed, but the `Authorization` and `Cookie` headers are still reflected in the response with Gafaelfawr tokens and cookies stripped.

schema.title: Response Get Anonymous Ingress Anonymous Get

get.description: Return metadata about the running application. This route is not exposed outside the cluster and therefore cannot be used by external clients.

Protected services redirect to this URL when the user is not authenticated to start the authentication process. The user will then be sent to an authentication provider, back to this URL with additional parameters to complete the process, and then back to the protected site.

parameters.name: code

schema.title: Provider code
schema.description: Set by the authentication provider after authentication

parameters.description: Set by the authentication provider after authentication

parameters.name: state

schema.title: Authentication state
schema.description: Set by the authentication provider after authentication to protect against session fixation

parameters.description: Set by the authentication provider after authentication to protect against session fixation

parameters.name: rd

schema.title: URL to return to
schema.description: User is sent here after successful authentication. Overrides `X-Auth-Request-Redirect` if both are set.

parameters.description: User is sent here after successful authentication. Overrides `X-Auth-Request-Redirect` if both are set.

parameters.name: x-auth-request-redirect

schema.title: URL to return to
schema.description: User is sent here after successful authentication

parameters.description: User is sent here after successful authentication

Location.description: URL of authentication provider or protected site

get.description:
Log out and redirect the user.

The user is redirected to the URL given in the rd parameter, if any, and
otherwise to the after_logout_url configuration setting.

If the user was logged in via GitHub (and Gafaelfawr is still configured
to use GitHub), the GitHub OAuth authorization grant is also revoked.

parameters.name: rd

schema.title: URL to return to
schema.description: User is sent here after operation

parameters.description: User is sent here after operation

get.description: Authenticates the user and then returns an authorization code to the OpenID Connect client via redirect. All errors except those from an invalid OpenID client ID are reported via a redirect back to the protected service with error and error_description set.

parameters.name: client_id

schema.title: Client ID
schema.description: Identifier of the registered OpenID Client

parameters.description: Identifier of the registered OpenID Client

parameters.name: redirect_uri

schema.title: URL to return to
schema.description: User is sent here after successful or failed authentication

parameters.description: User is sent here after successful or failed authentication

parameters.name: response_type

schema.title: Requested response type
schema.description: code is the only supported response type

parameters.description: code is the only supported response type

parameters.name: scope

schema.title: Requested token scopes
schema.description: Token scopes separated by spaces. The openid scope is required, and profile and email scopes are supported. All other scopes are ignored.

parameters.description: Token scopes separated by spaces. The openid scope is required, and profile and email scopes are supported. All other scopes are ignored.

parameters.name: state

schema.title: Opaque state cookie
schema.description: Set by the client to prevent session fixation attacks. Will be returned verbatim in the response. The client should verify that it matches the code sent in the request by, for example comparing it to a code set in a cookie.

parameters.description: Set by the client to prevent session fixation attacks. Will be returned verbatim in the response. The client should verify that it matches the code sent in the request by, for example comparing it to a code set in a cookie.

parameters.name: nonce

schema.title: ID token nonce
schema.description: Nonce to include in ID tokens to mitigate replay attacks or associate an ID token with a client session

parameters.description: Nonce to include in ID tokens to mitigate replay attacks or associate an ID token with a client session

307.description: Redirect for authentication or back to client

post.description: Redeem an authorization code for a token

get.summary: Get user metadata from OIDC token
get.description: Return information about the holder of a JWT

schema.title: Response Get Userinfo Auth Openid Userinfo Get

get.description: Returns the key set used for JWT signatures in the format specified in [RFC 7517](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7517) and [RFC 7518](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7518)
Returns OpenID Connect configuration information in the format specified in the [OpenID Connect Discovery 1.0](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html) specification.

scopes.title: All known scopes
scopes.description: All scopes currently recognized by the server.  Tokens may have other scopes, but new tokens may only be issued with one of these scopes.

APIConfig.title: APIConfig
APIConfig.description:
Configuration information for the API.

Supplemental information about the Gafaelfawr configuration that is useful
to a UI and therefore is returned as part of a login response.

csrf.title: CSRF token
csrf.description: This token must be included in any non-GET request using cookie authentication as the value of the ``X-CSRF-Token`` header

scopes.title: Access scopes
scopes.description: Access scopes for this authenticated user

APILoginResponse.title: APILoginResponse
APILoginResponse.description:
Response to an API login request.

The JavaScript UI visits the ``/auth/api/v1/login`` route to get a CSRF
token and metadata about the currently-authenticated user from the session
cookie (which the UI doesn't have the keys to read).

username.title: User for which to issue a token
username.description: The username may only contain lowercase letters, digits, and hyphen-minus, and may not start or end with a dash

token_name.title: User-given name of the token
token_name.description: Only provide this field for a token type of ``user``

expires.title: Token expiration
expires.description: Expiration timestamp of the token in seconds since epoch, or omitted to never expire

name.title: Preferred full name
name.description: If a value is not provided, and LDAP is configured, the full name from the LDAP entry for that username will be used

email.title: Email address
email.description: If a value is not provided, and LDAP is configured, the email address from the LDAP entry for that username will be used

uid.title: UID number
uid.description: If a value is not provided, and Firestore or LDAP are configured, the UID from Firestore (preferred) or the LDAP entry for that username will be used

gid.title: Primary GID
gid.description: GID of primary group. If set, should correspond to the id of a group of which the user is a member. If a value is not provided and LDAP is configured to add user private groups, it will be set to the same value as the UID.

groups.title: Groups
groups.description: Groups of which the user is a member. If a value is not provided, and LDAP is configured, the group membership from LDAP will be used

AdminTokenRequest.title: AdminTokenRequest
AdminTokenRequest.description: A request to create a new token via the admin interface.

AuthType.title: AuthType
AuthType.description: Authentication types for the WWW-Authenticate header.

grant_type.title: Request type
grant_type.description: `authorization_code` is the only supported grant type

redirect_uri.title: URL of client
redirect_uri.description: Must match `redirect_uri` in the client registration

ErrorDetail.title: ErrorDetail
ErrorDetail.description: The detail of the error message.

HealthCheck.title: HealthCheck
HealthCheck.description: Results of an internal health check.

HealthStatus.title: HealthStatus
HealthStatus.description:
Status of health check.

Since errors are returned as HTTP 500 errors, currently the only status is
the healthy status.

kid.title: Key ID
kid.description: A name for the key, also used in the header of a JWT signed by that key. Allows the signer to have multiple valid keys at a time and thus support key rotation.

n.title: RSA modulus
n.description: Big-endian modulus component of the RSA public key encoded in URL-safe base64 without trailing padding

e.title: RSA exponent
e.description: Big-endian exponent component of the RSA public key encoded in URL-safe base64 without trailing padding

JWK.title: JWK
JWK.description: The schema for a JSON Web Key (RFCs 7517 and 7518).

keys.title: Signing keys
keys.description: Valid signing keys for OpenID Connect JWTs

NewToken.title: NewToken
NewToken.description: Response to a token creation request.

spawn.title: Spawning allowed
spawn.description: Whether the user is allowed to spawn a notebook

NotebookQuota.title: NotebookQuota
NotebookQuota.description: Notebook Aspect quota information for a user.

response_types_supported.title: Supported response types
response_types_supported.description: ``code`` is the only supported response type

response_modes_supported.title: Supported response modes
response_modes_supported.description: ``query`` is the only supported response mode

grant_types_supported.title: Supported grant types
grant_types_supported.description: ``authorization_code`` is the only supported grant type

subject_types_supported.title: Supported subject types
subject_types_supported.description: ``public`` is the only supported subject type

id_token_signing_alg_values_supported.title: Supported JWT signing algorithms
id_token_signing_alg_values_supported.description: ``RS256`` is the only supported signing algorithm

OIDCErrorReply.title: OIDCErrorReply
OIDCErrorReply.description: An error from an OpenID Connect token request.

access_token.title: Authentication token
access_token.description: access_token and id_token are the same in this implementation

id_token.title: Identity token
id_token.description: access_token and id_token are the same in this implementation

scope.title: Scopes of token
scope.description: Scopes of the issued token, with any unrecognized or unauthorized scopes from the request filtered out

OIDCTokenReply.title: OIDCTokenReply
OIDCTokenReply.description: A reply to a successful OpenID Connect token request.

api.title: API quotas
api.description: Mapping of service names to allowed requests per minute.

groups.title: Quota grants by group
groups.description: Additional quota grants by group name

bypass.title: Groups without quotas
bypass.description: Groups whose members bypass all quota restrictions

groups.title: Quota grants by group
groups.description: Additional quota grants by group name

bypass.title: Groups without quotas
bypass.description: Groups whose members bypass all quota restrictions

Satisfy.title: Satisfy
Satisfy.description:
Authorization strategies.

Controls how to do authorization when there are multiple required scopes.
A strategy of ANY allows the request if the authentication token has any
of the required scopes.  A strategy of ALL requires that the
authentication token have all the required scopes.

description.title: Scope description
examples: Can create and modify user tokens

TapQuota.title: TapQuota
TapQuota.description: TAP quota information for a user.

TokenChange.title: TokenChange
TokenChange.description: Type of change made to a token.

token_name.title: Name of the token
token_name.description: Only set for tokens of type user. If the name was changed, this will be the new name of the token.

parent.title: Key of parent token of this token

service.title: Service to which the token was issued
service.description: Only set for tokens of type internal.

expires.title: Expiration of the token
expires.description: If the expiration was changed, this will be the new expiration of the token.

actor.title: Username of person making the change

action.title: Type of change that was made

old_token_name.title: Previous name of the token
old_token_name.description: This field will only be present for edit changes to user tokens that changed the token name.

old_scopes.title: Previous scopes of the token
old_scopes.description: This field will only be present for edit changes that changed the token scopes.

old_expires.title: Previous expiration of the token
old_expires.description: This field will only be present for edit changes that changed the expiration of the token.

ip_address.title: IP address from which the change was made
ip_address.description: May be null if the change was made internally, such as token deletion due to expiration.

TokenChangeHistoryEntry.title: TokenChangeHistoryEntry
TokenChangeHistoryEntry.description: A record of a change to a token.

username.title: Username
username.description: User to whom the token was issued

service.title: Service
service.description: Service to which the token was delegated. Only present for internal tokens.

created.title: Creation time
created.description: Creation timestamp of the token in seconds since epoch

expires.title: Expiration time
expires.description: Expiration timestamp of the token in seconds since epoch

last_used.title: Last used
last_used.description: When the token was last used in seconds since epoch

TokenInfo.title: TokenInfo
TokenInfo.description:
Information about a token.

All information about the token that's stored in the underlying
database. It includes some fields not present in Redis.

username.title: Username
username.description: User to whom the token was issued

gid.title: Primary GID
gid.description: GID of primary group. If set, this will also be the GID of one of the groups of which the user is a member.

groups.title: Groups
groups.description: Groups of which the user is a member

UserInfo.title: UserInfo
UserInfo.description:
Metadata about a user.

All user metadata from whatever source (admin request, GitHub, LDAP,
Firestore, etc.).

expires.title: Expiration time
expires.description: Expiration timestamp of the token in seconds since epoch, or None to never expire.

UserTokenModifyRequest.title: UserTokenModifyRequest
UserTokenModifyRequest.description:
The parameters of a user token that can be changed.

This is a separate model from `UserTokenRequest` because the
``token_name`` field is optional on modify requests.

expires.title: Expiration time
expires.description: Expiration timestamp of the token in seconds since epoch

UserTokenRequest.title: UserTokenRequest
UserTokenRequest.description: The parameters of a user token that are under the user's control.
